summary_one_line: A cHRM chunk whose declared length field is any value other than 32, taking the length-mismatch error branch.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A cHRM chunk (FOURCC 0x63 0x48 0x52 0x4d) must be present and reached, AND its 4-byte big-endian length field must hold a value != 32, so png_handle_cHRM takes the true side of `if (length != 32)` at pngrutil.c:1256.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog resolves 10/10 and hits the true side 16 times (DIVERGENT BRANCHES L1256 W T=16); its winning seeds carry the literal cHRM FOURCC bytes (offset 0x35-0x38 'cHRM') and reach the length compare, and I2S substitution can place a chunk-length value that is not 32 into the 4-byte length field that this exact compare reads.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: value_profile blocks 8/10 and never reaches the true side (DIVERGENT BRANCHES L1256 L T=0); without I2SRandReplace it only ever falls through with length==32 (the false side, T=0 F=12), so it cannot manufacture the precise length!=32 condition at this gate and stalls 20.60h.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace on top of value_profile's CMP_MAP feedback; I2S logs the (length, 32) compare operands and splices a non-32 length into the input bytes that feed `if (length != 32)`, deterministically flipping the gate to its true side, which value_profile's distance-bucket feedback alone cannot accomplish.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the length-mismatch compare in png_handle_cHRM whose true side is globally blocked.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
Source line:    if (length != 32)
Globally blocked side: T  (true branch)

evidence_trail.claim: The single decisive pair is value_profile_cmplog over value_profile with an I2S technique delta.
The winner hits the true side 16 times while the loser never hits it.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L1256  T=16 F=12  T=0 F=12  if (length != 32)  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Winning seeds carry the literal cHRM chunk FOURCC bytes near offset 0x35.
The chunk-type bytes at offsets 0x35-0x38 vary widely in the winner including the cHRM signature, while the loser is essentially fixed.
value_profile lacks the I2SRandReplace mutator that vpc adds.
value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace on top of the havoc/token stack.
The loser blocks 8/10 and stalls for 20.60h while the winner resolves 10/10.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 3970 --role W --fuzzer value_profile_cmplog --trial 1 --seed 199f550d26971022` and observed 2 I2S-floor dash rows (mutation_op = -) at depth 2 and depth 3 of the ancestor chain, the characteristic unwrapped-I2SRandReplace signature for vpc under the current build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic cHRM-style harness with `if (length != 32)` where value_profile resolves the length!=32 true side as fast as value_profile_cmplog (no I2S advantage), showing CMP_MAP distance feedback alone flips this exact-value gate.

weakest_evidence_point: The byte-diff does not isolate the 4-byte length field offset that feeds this compare, so the claim that I2S substitutes the length operand (rather than I2S merely planting the cHRM FOURCC to reach the gate) is inferred from source structure, not a pinpointed length-byte divergence.
